response:
An anonymous function in Python can be constructed using the lambda keyword. This function will check if a number is prime or composite using the modulus operator. The status of the number will be determined based on its divisibility. The status is updated as the function evaluates each divisor.

* * *

The modulus operator helps identify factors of the number efficiently. The status changes if any factor other than one and the number is found. The loop runs from two to the square root of the number. The status remains unchanged if no factors are found. The function returns the status as either prime or composite. The status is then used for further processing.

* * *

A prime number has only two distinct divisors. The status of the number is prime if no divisors are found. A composite number has more than two divisors. The status of the number is composite if divisors are found. The function provides a clear status for each input. The status is returned without additional explanation.
